label: Self-attention

node.label: Self-attention
node.definition: Each position forms content-dependent weighted combinations of other positions in the same sequence.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Self-attention is modeled as depending on Embeddings & distributed representations, Sequences, events & time series and feeding downstream nodes such as Cross-attention & co-attention, Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention, Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms.
examples: atlas downstream link to Cross-attention & co-attention
atlas downstream link to Sparse, local, linear & recurrent attention
atlas downstream link to Relative position, RoPE, ALiBi & coordinate mechanisms
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Embeddings & distributed representations, Sequences, events & time series.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Self-attention has a `uses` dependency on Embeddings & distributed representations because Embeddings & distributed representations is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
In this atlas graph, Self-attention has a `uses` dependency on Sequences, events & time series because Sequences, events & time series is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
Self-attention is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component Affine transforms & dense layers.
In this atlas graph, Self-attention has a `uses` dependency on Probability theory because Probability theory is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.
